name: Art Baron
abstract: Arthur John Baron (born January 5, 1950)  is an American jazz trombonist. He also plays didgeridoo, conch shell, penny-whistle, alto and bass recorder, and tuba.

concerts.label: The New York Jazz Repertory Company: Duke Ellington 75th Birthday Salute
Kool Jazz Festival: Ellington's Black, Brown and Beige
JVC Jazz Festival: An Evening with Mel Tormé
Zankel Hall Opening Festival, Program 3: Family Concert: Brooklyn Youth Chorus

about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee is Art Baron himself. The transcript mentions 'Arthur Baron' in a biographical note and the interviewer refers to him as 'Art Baron'. There is no mention of another person named Art Baron with whom he has a relationship.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Blue
about_quotes.evidence: Joey Morant was there and Art Baron on trombone... The stories these cats had, and you learn man, it’s all about learning.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Blue interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 23, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Bergonzi
about_quotes.evidence: I moved into Manhattan with Art Baron who was on the road with Duke Ellington’s band... And we could play music 24/7.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Bergonzi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 7, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call from Art Baron that he recommended me for a Wynton Marsalis Lincoln Center concert
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: We have Britt Woodman, Art Baron, Jerry Dodgion and Harry Allen.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997
